controls.linetension_null: junctions require elastic interaction (glissile/Hirth anneal 1.00->no reaction); collinear annihilation collision-driven, tau_c 175->100 w/o elastic
controls.rann_maxseg: converged for MAXSEG>=80 (collinear 180, Hirth 100), robust to rann 5/10/20; MAXSEG=40 coarse-edge differs
controls.box: collinear tau_c=180 at L/Lseg=4,6,8 -> NOT a spanB>0.45*LBOX artifact
controls.orientation: 6 collinear families tau_c=160+/-20 (12%), all annihilate -> not a single-geometry fluke

conclusion: Controlled binary reactions confirm the correct mechanisms and collinear remobilization scaling, but the local remobilization matrix does NOT reproduce collinear dominance. Collinear dominance is therefore a collective forest/MFP effect -> measured in evolving_forest_mfp.
